steps.title: Find the time period from the exponent structure
steps.content:
The exponent is $4x$ where $x$ is measured in years. To find what time period the base 1.004 represents, we need the exponent to equal 1:
$$4x = 1 \implies x = \frac{1}{4} \text{ year}$$
Converting: $\frac{1}{4}$ year $= 3$ months. So the growth factor 1.004 applies to every 3-month interval.

steps.title: Interpret the growth factor 1.004
steps.content:
A balance multiplied by 1.004 can be rewritten as:
$$1.004 = 1 + 0.004 = 1 + 0.4\%$$
This means each new balance equals the old balance plus 0.4% of the old balance. In other words, every 3-month interval, the account balance increases by exactly 0.4% of its value at the start of that interval.

steps.title: Match to the correct choice
steps.content:
Evaluate each option:
  • Choice A — Every 3 months, the balance increases by about \$4. Claims fixed dollar increases. This contradicts exponential growth, which produces percentage increases.
  • Choice C — Every 4 years, the balance increases by about \$4. Also claims fixed dollar increases and misreads the exponent as a 4-year period.
  • Choice D — At the end of every 4-year interval, the balance increases by about 0.4% of the balance at the beginning of the 4-year interval. Claims a 0.4% increase every 4 years. But at $x = 4$ years, the exponent is $4(4) = 16$, giving factor $(1.004)^{16} \approx 1.066 \approx 6.6\%$ increase--not 0.4%.
  • Choice B — At the end of every 3-month interval, the balance increases by about 0.4% of the balance at the beginning of the 3-month interval. This is exactly what we derived above. ✓
That is the correct answer.
